tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, bilirubinuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), suggesting possible renal dysfunction and/or hepatic involvement. Low urine specific gravity may indicate impaired concentrating ability. Despite normal serum glucose, there is glucosuria and low HbA1c, which may reflect renal glycosuria or analytical interference. Further nephrological and hepatological evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
